dailyNews.description: Vance said UFOs weren't aliens, describing this as a Christian understanding of ‘celestial beings who fly around who do weird things to people.’

dailyNews.title: Bank of America to pay $72.5 million to settle lawsuits brought in by Epstein accusers
dailyNews.description: Lawyers for the bank and the women had told Manhattan-based U.S. District Judge Jed Rakoff this month they had reached a “settlement in principle,” but terms of the deal were not disclosed at the time.

dailyNews.title: Intense bombing in Tehran, Isfahan
dailyNews.description: Black smoke is rising over Tehran and Isfahan after early morning US-Israeli airstrikes on the cities.

dailyNews.title: Too loud? Ticket’s in the mail
dailyNews.description: Noise cameras are spreading across U.S. cities, issuing automatic tickets up to $2,500. NYC has already collected nearly $2 million in fines since 2021.

dailyNews.title: House Republicans pass rival DHS plan, setting up Senate fight as shutdown set to become longest in history
dailyNews.description: House Republicans passed a two-month DHS funding extension, but the 42-day partial government shutdown remains far from over amid continued gridlock.

dailyNews.title: Tiger Woods released from jail after DUI arrest; eyes appear bloodshot in booking photo
dailyNews.description: Tiger Woods was released from jail Friday night after several hours after he was arrested for driving under the influence following a car crash near his Florida home.

dailyNews.title: Controversial viral influencer 'Clavicular' arrested in Florida on battery charges
dailyNews.description: "Looksmaxxing" influencer Braden Peters known as "Clavicular" arrested and facing battery charges after alleged Airbnb incident in February.

dailyNews.title: At least 2 students killed, several injured in school bus crash in Tennessee: 'A parent's worst nightmare'
dailyNews.description: Two students were killed and seven were injured in a school bus crash on Highway 70 in Carroll County, Tennessee, during a Kenwood Middle School field trip.

dailyNews.title: "Face the Nation with Margaret Brennan" guests for March 29, 2026
dailyNews.description: This week's guests include White House border czar Tom Homan, Democratic Rep. Jim Himes, and former U.S. Surgeon General Dr. Jerome Adams.

dailyNews.title: 2/20: The Takeout with Major Garrett
dailyNews.description: Supreme Court strikes down Trump's emergency tariffs; U.S. and Canada to face off for gold medal in men's hockey

dailyNews.title: Iran claims Strait of Hormuz is closed as Trump says talks are underway
dailyNews.description: According to a statement from Iran's Revolutionary Guards, the Strait of Hormuz is closed, and they vowed a severe response to any ship trying to get through. That seemed to contradict President Trump's claim that Iran had allowed 10 ships through the Strait as a gift. Holly Williams has details.

dailyNews.title: House GOP attempts to forge own path toward ending shutdown, disregarding Senate-backed bill
dailyNews.description: House lawmakers are at odds over a Senate bill that includes TSA pay, but excludes ICE funding. Eleanor Mueller, White House economic policy reporter for Semafor, and Riley Rogerson, House leadership reporter for Politico, join "The Takeout" to discuss.

dailyNews.title: At CPAC, the ‘America First’ Crowd Ponders Giving War a Chance
dailyNews.description: The Conservative Political Action Conference has been an America First gathering for years, but with President Trump pursuing war in Iran, organizers have tried to make a shift, with mixed results.

dailyNews.title: Trump Offers More Aid to Farmers, a Key Support Bloc Hurt by Tariffs and War
dailyNews.description: The president announced new loan guarantees at a White House event that appeared intended to reassure a group that has found itself caught in the crosshairs of some of his major policies.

dailyNews.title: Outside Kennedy Center, Jane Fonda and Joan Baez Raise Voices in Protest
dailyNews.description: The pair joined in a gathering of artists and others who denounced censorship and faulted President Trump’s growing influence over the nation’s cultural life.

dailyNews.title: Kennedy Center Performer Asks Judge to Toss Case He Calls ‘Retaliatory’
dailyNews.description: A jazz drummer who called off a performance in protest of the center’s being renamed for President Trump said the president’s allies had sued him as punishment.

dailyNews.title: Trump Says Colombia Will Accept Deportees, Ending Tariff Standoff
dailyNews.description: The president has made immigration an early priority to start second term, with ICE making more than 900 arrests Sunday.

dailyNews.title: Trump Says He Wants to 'Clean Out' Gaza, Send Refugees to Egypt and Jordan
dailyNews.description: The president, calling the heavily bombed enclave “a demolition site,” proposed relocating Palestinians temporarily or long-term, a move rejected by Arab countries since the war began.

dailyNews.title: Beijing Signals Readiness to Talk to Trump's Team, Even Old Foes
dailyNews.description: The Chinese foreign minister spoke by phone with Marco Rubio, the new secretary of state who has been a critic of Beijing.

dailyNews.title: Canada Is Becoming a Fentanyl Exporter, and a Target for Trump
dailyNews.description: The drug is increasingly manufactured in and trafficked from the U.S.’s northern neighbor, though the numbers remain small compared with Mexico.
